data.title: Quantum Computing News: Federal Funding Push Signals Quantum's Shift Toward Commercial Use
data.description:
Welcome back to this week's Monday edition of quantum computing update. With the year now fully underway, the focus has returned to steady progress across science, policy, and early market use. As always, this review highlights the most relevant signals for investors tracking quantum systems over time.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In today's edition, we cover the ongoing debate around quantum advantage, new U.S. policy action, recent hardware updates from public firms, and fresh moves linking quantum tools with live business models.

Let us begin.

Quantum Advantage Debate Continues

First, the discussion around quantum advantage has shifted but not faded. Many researchers now agree that quantum machines have completed tasks that classic computers cannot finish at scale. These tasks involve random circuit sampling and are meant to test raw system power.

However, doubts remain as the tasks lack direct use and rely on proxy checks. Even so, this aligns with the original goal: to show a clear limit rather than deliver value today. As a result, the debate now centers on when usefulness should matter, not whether the first line was crossed.

This helps explain why firms like Alphabet (GOOGL), International Business Machines Corporation (IBM), and Microsoft (MSFT) maintain a long-term view of returns as progress continues.

U.S. Lawmakers Renew Quantum Support

Next, policy support in the U.S. is gaining pace. Lawmakers introduced the National Quantum Initiative Reauthorization Act to extend federal funding through 2034. The bill aims to link research with early use while also preparing for future security risks.

The plan expands work at the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) and the National Science Foundation (NSF) and adds NASA for the first time. It also calls for new research centers, job programs, and supply chain planning.

Public support came from IBM, Microsoft, Alphabet, and IonQ (IONQ).

Rigetti Delays Larger System for Quality Gains

Meanwhile, hardware progress continues at a careful pace. Rigetti Computing (RGTI) shared an update on its 108-qubit system built from smaller units. The launch was moved to early 2026 to improve gate quality.

The company said two-qubit accuracy is near 99%, with a target of 99.5% before release. This delay reflects a broader focus on reliability rather than speed across the sector.

Since Rigetti announced the delay, RGTI shares fell more than 1% in premarket trading.

Spectral Capital Links Quantum and Telecom

Finally, Spectral Capital Corporation (FCCN), an accelerator and investment firm that acquires and develops early-stage companies with advanced AI and quantum-related technologies, announced plans to acquire an Italy-based telecom firm. The deal aims to blend AI and quantum tools into live network systems that already produce cash flow.

The company said the target could deliver up to $10 million in annual revenue once fully integrated. While early, the move shows how some firms are tying quantum ideas to real business paths.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to line up all the quantum stocks mentioned in the piece alongside other notable ones. It's a quick way to see how they stack up and where the field could be heading.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Standard Chartered Prepares for a Major Crypto Leap with New Prime Brokerage
data.description:
The race to own the future of digital money is accelerating in 2026. Standard Chartered (SCBFF) is reportedly working on a new service called a prime brokerage for crypto. This would act as a one-stop shop for big funds and institutions that want to trade assets like Bitcoin (BTC-USD) but need a safe, professional way to do it. The project, known as Project37C, is being built inside the bank's venture capital arm, SC Ventures. This move shows that the London-based bank wants to be the main bridge between traditional finance and the new world of digital assets.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Standard Chartered Uses a Smart Way to Grow Fast

Standard Chartered is being very clever about how it builds this new business. By housing it inside SC Ventures instead of the main bank, they can avoid some very tough rules. Usually, big banks have to keep a massive amount of cash on hand if they hold Bitcoin, which makes it very expensive to run. Because this new branch is a venture unit, it doesn't have to follow those same heavy Basel III rules. This allows the bank to offer market access and trading tools much faster and more cheaply than its rivals.

Other Wall Street Banks Become Crypto-Friendly

Standard Chartered isn't alone in this push. Ever since the U.S. government became more friendly toward crypto last year, the biggest banks in the world have been rushing in. JPMorgan (JPM) is thinking about opening its own crypto trading desk, and Morgan Stanley (MS) is already launching new crypto funds. A big reason for this is the $140 billion that has flowed into Bitcoin ETFs over the last two years. Standard Chartered already lets big clients trade Bitcoin and Ether (ETH-USD), but this new brokerage will add extra services like lending and better risk management.

The bottom line is that this new project is part of a larger trend to make crypto trading look more like the regular stock market. Other big companies like Ripple (XRP-USD) have also been buying up prime brokers to help professionalize the industry. These moves are helping to turn crypto from a risky experiment into a standard part of global finance.

Investors can track the prices of their favorite cryptos on the TipRanks Cryptocurrency Center. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/12/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Plunge on Trump-Powell Tensions, Big Bank Earnings Ahead
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down early Monday as investors reacted to President Donald Trump's subpoena of Federal Chair Jerome Powell. Markets are also getting ready for the Q4 earnings season, which begins this week with major banks reporting first.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 1.05%, 0.73%, and 0.60%, respectively, at 3:41 a.m. EST on January 12.

During Friday's regular trading, all three major indexes closed higher, with the S&P 500 and the 30-stock Dow closing at record highs. Despite a volatile trading week, the indexes ended in positive territory. The S&P 500 climbed over 1%, the Dow advanced 2.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite increased 1.9%.

Looking ahead, investors will closely watch key economic reports, including the important Consumer Price Index (CPI) data due on Tuesday, Producer Price Index (PPI) data, retail sales, and weekly initial jobless claims.

Moreover, the fourth-quarter earnings season is set to open, with major banks reporting this week. Companies reporting include JPMorgan (JPM), Delta Air Lines (DAL), Bank of America (BAC), Citigroup (C), Wells Fargo (WFC), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Goldman Sachs (GS), and Morgan Stanley (MS).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.19%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $59.07 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,583 per ounce on Monday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mostly lower on Monday amid heightened geopolitical tensions related to Iran and Greenland.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Higher

Asia-Pacific markets traded higher on January 12, after U.S. President Donald Trump's comments to intervene in Iran's ongoing unrest.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index surged 1.44%. In China, the Shanghai Composite gained 1.09%, while the Shenzhen Component added 2.40%. Meanwhile, Japan's markets remained closed for a public holiday.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Walmart Stock (WMT) Rallies on Drone Delivery Expansion, AI Shopping Plans
data.description:
Shares of America's big box retailer Walmart (WMT) are rallying in pre-market trading on plans to expand drone delivery to about 150 more stores by the end of 2027, reaching a total of 270 stores. Walmart is currently offering the service in partnership with Alphabet's (GOOGL) Wing and Zipline, to select areas in Dallas-Fort Worth, Atlanta, Northwest Arkansas and Charlotte, N.C.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Walmart also plans to roll out Google's Gemini assistant for effortless discovery and purchases for Walmart and Sam's Club shoppers. This follows its October deal with OpenAI's ChatGPT, which introduced "Instant Checkout," allowing buyers to purchase items without leaving the chatbot. OpenAI has similar deals with Etsy (ETSY) and Shopify (SHOP) merchants like Skims, Vuori, and Spanx.

Why Is Drone Delivery Important?

Drones enable short, rapid deliveries with lightweight payloads, emphasizing speed and convenience for time-sensitive needs. Wing's drones carry up to 5 pounds over 6-mile round trips, delivering small tethered boxes in yards within 30 minutes. Customers usually opt for drone delivery for last-minute essentials like ingredients or medicine, with 25% of customers using the service three times weekly. Walmart offers it free to Walmart+ members and charges $19.99 to other customers.

The retailer aims to serve millions with last-minute drone orders. However, requirements like human spotters for each drone have limited growth, alongside complaints over noise, safety, privacy, and weather constraints.

This planned expansion will reach over 40 million Walmart shoppers, up from 2 million today. New markets include Los Angeles, Houston, Cincinnati, St. Louis, and Florida cities like Orlando, Tampa, and Miami. Competitors such as Amazon (AMZN) and DoorDash (DASH) also offer drone delivery in select areas.

Walmart's Push to Capture Chatbot-Driven Shopping

Walmart views the shift to AI-chatbot-initiated searches as a pivotal evolution in consumer habits. U.S. ecommerce chief David Guggina stated it drives a digital strategy to "meet customers earlier" through agentic AI. These agents streamline need discovery across platforms, reducing reliance on Walmart's app and website. These AI agents will eventually change the workforce and employees' roles, boosting efficiency and scale.

Is Walmart Stock a Buy?

On TipRanks, WMT stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 25 Buys and one Hold rating. The average Walmart price target of $125.75 implies 9.8% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, WMT shares have surged 25%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Gold Price Hits Another Record on Fed Independence Fears
data.description:
The gold price soared to another record high today as investors fretted about alleged U.S. government interference in the independence of the Federal Reserve.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gold futures jumped 2% to $4,592.90 per ounce in early trading, while spot gold surged 1.8% to $4,590.

Safest of Havens

Investors sought out the precious metal as a safe haven on news that a criminal probe had been opened into the Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell.

Powell revealed at the weekend that the Justice Department served the central bank with grand jury subpoenas, threatening a criminal indictment related to the Chair's testimony before the U.S. Senate.

"The threat of criminal charges is a consequence of the Federal Reserve setting interest rates based on our best assessment of what will serve the public, rather than following the preferences of the president," Powell said.

Investors took fright on what this could mean for the Fed moving forward.

"Investors feared about the independence of the Federal Reserve," said Russ Mould, investment director at AJ Bell. "The probe has unnerved markets and raised questions about what might happen to the Fed once Powell steps down in May. There is a fear that President Trump is meddling too much with policies that are meant to be set independently. Gold is an asset with safe-haven qualities that often shines in an uncertain market."

Fears over Iran Conflict

Geopolitical fears were also behind the surge in the gold price. This is mainly centered around the recent events in Iran where hundreds of people have reportedly been killed in anti-government protests. Trump has said that the U.S. is considering some "very strong options" in Iran, with the leaders in Tehran in turn threatening Israel and U.S. interests in the region.

"Escalating rhetoric between Iran and the U.S., alongside revelations that chair Powell faced threats from the White House over past decisions, heightened the sense of political risk,"  said Matt Britzman, senior equity analyst at Hargreaves Lansdown. "A softer U.S. jobs print on Friday added fuel to the gold trade, with traders leaning into expectations of two rate cuts this year ahead of a pivotal inflation report later this week."
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Visa (V), Mastercard (MA) Stocks Fall on Trump's Proposed 10% Credit Interest Cap
data.description:
Shares of credit card giants Visa (V) and Mastercard (MA) fell in pre-market trading after President Donald Trump proposed a one-year cap of 10% on credit card interest rates. Although the cap is aimed at benefitting consumers amid economic pressures, card companies worry it would squeeze their earnings. These companies make money from three sources, namely merchant fees, customer fees, and interest on balances. Some researchers and left-leaning policymakers argue that banks could remain profitable from merchant fees alone, even with the cap.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Trump posted about the idea on his Truth Social (DJT) account Friday but was unclear whether it would come via executive order or new legislation. A Republican senator confirmed he had spoken with Trump, who fully backs a bill and wants it ready by January 20, exactly a year after he took office.

Banks, Credit Card Companies Oppose the Plan

Wall Street and credit card companies, major donors to Trump's 2024 campaign and allies in his second term, would likely oppose the proposal. Banks warn that the cap would harm low-income people most by reducing credit access, forcing them toward costlier options like payday loans or pawnshops.

Meanwhile, researchers at the Vanderbilt Policy Accelerator, who analyzed Trump's campaign pledge last year, estimate that Americans could save about $100 billion annually in interest with a 10% cap. The industry would take a significant hit but stay profitable, likely leading to smaller credit card rewards and perks.

According to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, about 195 million Americans held credit cards in 2024 and paid $160 billion in interest. Credit card debt reached a record $1.23 trillion in Q3 2025, as per the New York Federal Reserve. Average rates now range from 19.65% to 21.5%, down slightly from recent Fed rate cuts but near 30-year highs, and up from about 12% a decade ago.

The proposal does not appear to conflict with the CARD Act, enacted during the 2008 financial crisis, which curbs abusive practices like retroactive rate hikes on existing balances. It could in fact align with the act by expanding consumer safeguards temporarily.

Visa or Mastercard: Which Is the Better Card Stock?

According to the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool, analysts have assigned both Visa and Mastercard shares a "Strong Buy" consensus rating. However, MA stock offers a slightly higher upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Is Sliding in Pre-Market Today — 1/12/25
data.description:
Shares of Nvidia (NVDA) slipped in pre-market trading on Monday as investors reviewed demand in China and the broader outlook for AI stocks. NVDA stock fell about 1.3% ahead of the open, extending recent weakness after CES news on robotics and self-driving failed to lift the stock. Year-to-date, NVDA stock is down by 2.57%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Adding to the caution, the recent surge in Chinese AI stocks—and growing talk that China's tech sector could outperform U.S. peers by 2026—has also weighed on investor sentiment. Still, top analysts remain confident in Nvidia's core AI chip business, pointing to strong demand from a wide range of customers and its dominant market position.

Upcoming Catalysts for NVDA Stock in 2026

A key catalyst for the stock could be signs that Chinese companies are moving ahead with purchases of Nvidia's H200 chips. CEO Jensen Huang said at CES that demand for the China-focused chip is "very strong" and that he does not expect problems from regulators.

According to Reuters, Nvidia already has orders for more than two million H200 chips, priced at about $27,000 each—equal to roughly $54 billion in potential revenue. However, Chinese authorities have asked some tech firms to temporarily pause orders while they review how many domestically made chips should be bought alongside Nvidia's products.

The latest approval by the Trump administration unexpectedly gave Chinese companies access to the H200 chip, which is far more powerful than the previously approved H20. For many Chinese firms, the H200 is now the most advanced chip they can legally buy. However, shipments to China are not guaranteed. Chinese regulators have not yet formally approved imports of the H200 chip, and sources say officials are holding urgent discussions to review its potential impact.

What's Next for Investors?

NVDA stock could get a lift from strong demand in China if H200 shipments are approved, but regulatory uncertainty keeps the upside in check.

For investors, the key drivers are sustained AI chip demand, upcoming product launches, and signals from China. Continued data center growth could fuel further gains, while high valuation and rising competition remain the main risks.

In addition, investors may get a clearer view of the AI chip market this week when Nvidia's key supplier, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, or TSMC, (TSM), reports earnings on Thursday. The results could act as another catalyst for NVDA stock.

What Is the Target Price of Nvidia Stock?

According to TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $264.97, Nvidia's average share price target implies a 43.34% upside potential.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: U.S. Banks See Their Strongest Investment Banking Momentum in Years
data.description:
U.S. banks are witnessing their strongest investment banking period in years, with 2025 on track to be their best since the pandemic. According to Bloomberg data, the five largest U.S. investment‑banking players, JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Goldman Sachs (GS), Morgan Stanley (MS), Bank of America (BAC), and Citigroup (C), are expected to report nearly $10 billion in investment banking revenue for the latest quarter. This reflects about a 13% jump from the same period last year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

If those numbers hold, the group's full‑year 2025 investment banking revenue would be nearly $38 billion. This marks a major recovery from the 2023 low of $25 billion and the strongest result since 2021.

Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, the two banks most dependent on investment banking fees, are likely to post some of the biggest gains. Analysts estimate their 2025 fees will be at least 17% higher than last year. Also, both banks are expected to see another 11% increase in 2026.

It is worth mentioning that JPMorgan kicks off earnings on Tuesday, followed by Bank of America, Citi, and Wells Fargo (WFC) on Wednesday. Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley report on Thursday.

Factors Aiding Investment Banking Rebound

A major driver of investment banking activity in the fourth quarter of 2025 was a resurgence in corporate confidence. Companies that had delayed mergers, acquisitions, and capital‑raising during the volatile post-pandemic years finally returned to the market.

At the same time, equity markets were strong, creating a more favorable backdrop for IPOs and secondary offerings. Together, these trends helped rebuild pipelines that had been thin since 2022.

Also, a more supportive regulatory environment contributed to the rebound. With U.S. policymakers easing certain post-crisis constraints, banks had more flexibility to deploy capital and support client transactions.

Overall, stronger markets, more companies willing to do deals, and better visibility into 2026 pipelines created the most supportive environment banks had seen in years.

data.title: 'The Gap Is Still Wide,' Says Top Analyst about Nvidia Stock
data.description:
This article was written by Shalu Saraf and reviewed by Gilan Miller-Gertz.

Top Baird analyst Tristan Gerra sees Nvidia (NVDA) maintaining a clear lead in artificial intelligence, based on the latest industry checks. In a recent note, Gerra, a five-star analyst, reiterated his Outperform rating on the stock and kept a $275 price target, again naming Nvidia a "top idea for 2026."

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It is worth noting that Gerra ranks 385 out of more than 10,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He has a success rate of 56%, with an average return per rating of 16.20% over a one-year timeframe.

Will Nvidia Keep its AI lead?

Gerra said Nvidia's edge goes well beyond selling chips. He believes the company is building full AI ecosystems that bring together hardware, software, and tools on one platform. As AI systems grow more complex, he said this makes it more likely for customers to stay with Nvidia rather than switch to newer or less proven options.

The analyst also highlighted Nvidia's massive developer base as a key advantage. Around seven million developers are now building on Nvidia's platforms, which Gerra said raises switching costs and creates a strong barrier for rivals trying to gain share.

Some investors worry that competition could rise as AI inference becomes a larger part of demand over time. Gerra pushed back on that view, noting that many in the industry expect Nvidia to retain a far-leading share in AI even as inference grows much larger starting in 2028. In his view, Nvidia's platform depth puts it in a strong position across both training and inference.

Also, Gerra said industry checks and insights from Nvidia's recent CES presentation support his outlook. He believes Nvidia's early lead, combined with its broad ecosystem, continues to give the company a clear advantage as AI adoption expands.

Is Nvidia a Buy or Sell Stock?  

According to TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $264.97, the Nvidia average share price target implies almost 43% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: S&P 500 Gains May Slow in 2026 — but Raymond James Says These 2 Stocks Could Beat the Market
data.description:
After posting a 16% gain last year, the S&P 500 capped off its third straight year of double-digit returns. But can 2026 deliver another solid year for equities, or is the market due for a cooldown?

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That's the debate taking shape on Wall Street – and according to Raymond James Chief Investment Officer Larry Adam, the pace of gains may begin to slow.

"Valuations for the S&P 500 have climbed to the 95th percentile, meaning price-to-earnings multiple expansion has little more to give," Adam opined. "Returns will need to rely squarely on earnings growth. The good news? We expect earnings to rise about 12%, supporting our year-end S&P 500 target of 7,250. That means the return profile shifts from double-digit gains to mid-single digits. That said, conditions remain favorable: solid economic growth, an easing Fed, stable long-term rates, and healthy buybacks and dividends. Compared to recent years, however, you'll want to keep expectations in check for equity returns."

For investors aiming to go beyond that baseline and hunt for outsized upside, the Raymond James analysts have pinpointed two stocks they believe could meaningfully outperform the market in 2026. Both are rated as Strong Buys by the firm, and to put those convictions in context, we turned to the TipRanks database to see how the broader Street stacks up behind the calls. Let's dive in.

Darling Ingredients (DAR)

Up first on our radar is Darling Ingredients, a company that has multiple lines of business, including food tech, agribusiness, and waste recycling. Darling's core business is collecting the waste products of the animal husbandry, agriculture, and food industries and recycling them for use in a wide range of areas: as feedstock and food products, including animal feeds and human food additives, and as biofuels.

Darling has split its business into three branches: feed, food, and fuel. The first of these includes fats and proteins that are essential nutritious animal feeds and are used in both livestock and pet foods. Darling also uses its feedstock fats in its biofuel program. The company boasts that its rendering business is the largest in the world.

On the food side, Darling is a world leader in making and distributing collagens and gelatins. These products are produced in an international network of 16 factories. The gelatins have a vital role in the food industry as stabilizers, clarifiers, and thickeners, while the collagens are used in the health and wellness sectors.

Last month, Darling signed a definitive agreement with Belgium-based Tessenderlo, an industrial company with a strong footprint in the same feed and feedstock fields where Darling works. The two companies will combine their collagen and gelatin segments into a new firm to be called Nextida. Darling will hold an 85% stake in the new venture, which will not require a cash investment from either parent.

The last segment of Darling's business, fuel, is home to the company's biodiesel, biogas, and aviation fuels. These fuels are mainly produced by recycling cooking oil and animal fats, with the biogas being generated from food waste and pig manure. Darling's fuel business is designed to produce a vital product from otherwise dangerous biological waste products.

On the financial side, Darling last reported results for 3Q25. In that quarter, the company's top line came to $1.6 billion – a figure that beat the forecast by almost $62 million and was up 14% year-over-year. The company's bottom line, the 12-cent GAAP EPS, was 11 cents per share lower than expected. We should note that at the end of 3Q25, Darling had $91.5 million in available liquid assets, compared to $75.9 million at the end of December 2024.

This stock has caught the eye of Raymond James's Justin Jenkins, an analyst ranked among the top 1% on Wall Street, who lays out an upbeat take. The 5-star analyst writes, "RD and related sustainable fuels represent a structural growth element for DAR and industry peers – with pivotal regulatory catalysts (i.e., RVO finalization, SREs, Producer tax credits/45z) in the pipeline that can further solidify our positive outlook. This is all while DAR's core 'upstream' operations remain a steady cash flow generator, and the stock price is nearing a point where the DGD JV stake is a call option value. All told, we think the business is at/near a trough for fundamentals and sentiment, and expect a solid recovery in earnings and the trading multiple… especially into 2026+."

Jenkins gives DAR a Strong Buy rating, with a $60 price target that implies a 53.5% gain in the coming months. (To watch Jenkins' track record, click here)

The 9 recent analyst reviews here are unanimous, for a Strong Buy consensus rating. The stock's $39.08 trading price and $48.89 average target price together indicate a 25% gain in store on the one-year timeframe. (See DAR stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus
